title: So / Such ... that & Too / Enough
description: Intensificadores con resultado: 'The movie was so boring that I fell asleep.' 'She is old enough to decide.' 'It's too hot to go outside.'

items.front: The movie was so boring that I fell asleep. / It was such a beautiful day that we went to the beach.
items.back: La película era tan aburrida que me quedé dormido. / Era un día tan lindo que fuimos a la playa.
items.explanation: SO + adjetivo o adverbio + that. SUCH + (a/an) + adjetivo + sustantivo + that. Ambos expresan intensidad con una consecuencia. REGLA: so + adj/adv (so tired, so quickly). SUCH + noun phrase (such a mess, such nice people). Con plurales/incontables no hay 'a': 'such nice people', 'such good weather'.

cardRef.front: So / Such ... that (so + adjective/adverb + that | such + (a/an) + adjective + noun + that)

items.front: This suitcase is too heavy for me to carry. / She is old enough to make her own decisions.
items.back: Esta maleta es demasiado pesada para que yo la cargue. / Es suficientemente grande para tomar sus propias decisiones.
items.explanation: TOO + adjetivo = exceso que IMPIDE algo. Tiene sentido negativo. ENOUGH va DESPUÉS de adjetivos/adverbios pero ANTES de sustantivos: 'big enough' pero 'enough chairs'. Se puede agregar 'for + persona': 'too heavy FOR ME to carry', 'old enough FOR HER to decide'.

steps.title: So... that y such... that
steps.instruction: Ambos expresan intensidad + resultado. SO + adj/adv + that. SUCH + (a/an) + adj + noun + that. Si hay un sustantivo en la frase, usá 'such'. Si es solo adjetivo/adverbio, usá 'so'.

steps.title: Too (demasiado) y enough (suficiente)
steps.instruction: TOO = exceso que impide: 'too tired TO sleep'. ENOUGH = suficiencia: 'old enough TO vote'. La posición de 'enough' cambia: AFTER adjective/adverb, BEFORE noun.

steps.title: Completá con el intensificador correcto
steps.instruction: Elegí so, such, too o enough según la estructura.

exercises.template: He spoke {0} quickly that I couldn't understand him.
It was {0} a great performance that the audience stood up.
The coffee is {0} hot to drink right now.
She is not old {0} to vote in the election.

steps.title: ¿Error o correcta?
steps.instruction: Identificá errores en el uso de so/such/too/enough.

exercises.sentence: It was a so good movie that I watched it twice.
exercises.correction: It was such a good movie that I watched it twice.
exercises.explanation: INCORRECTO. Cuando hay un SUSTANTIVO (movie) en la frase, usamos SUCH, no SO. Correcto: 'such a good movie'. Si no hubiera sustantivo: 'The movie was so good that...'

exercises.sentence: The food was so delicious that we ordered more.
exercises.explanation: Correcto. SO + adjetivo (delicious, sin sustantivo directo después) + that + resultado. Estructura perfecta.

exercises.sentence: He's too young for drive a car.
exercises.correction: He's too young to drive a car.
exercises.explanation: INCORRECTO. Con TOO: too + adj + TO + infinitivo (base verb). No 'for + infinitivo'. 'Too young TO drive', 'too hot TO drink'.

exercises.sentence: She has enough experience to apply for the promotion.
exercises.explanation: Correcto. ENOUGH + sustantivo (enough experience). 'Enough' va ANTES del sustantivo. Y luego 'to + infinitivo' para el resultado.

steps.title: ¡Lección completa!
steps.instruction: SO + adj/adv + that (so tired that, so quickly that). SUCH + (a/an) + adj + noun + that (such a big problem, such nice weather). TOO + adj + TO + infinitivo (demasiado para — sentido negativo). adj/adv + ENOUGH + TO + infinitivo (suficientemente para). ENOUGH + noun (enough money, enough time — before noun). Regla de oro: sustantivo → such. Solo adj/adv → so.
